mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) translates into higher aggregate wage income and consumer spending, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Nonfarm payrolls are a coincident indicator of aggregate demand
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.5% increases the cost of corporate borrowing and reduces the present value of future earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand and labor market deterioration, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term between RSAFS and UNRATE remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction does not add incremental explanatory power over single-proxy models (R-squared improvement < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: In post-recession recovery periods, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects supply-chain normalization and inventory rebuilding, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods classified as post-recession recovery, positive month-over-month INDPRO changes do not correlate with significantly positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) raises inflation expectations and discount rates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and decreases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirm labor market tightening, which boosts wage-income expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease does not produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,200 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and construction activity, which drags on aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in less than 45% of triggered instances when HOUST < 1200.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.
